gap_summary.notes: No blocking gap requires new scraping before chart planning. Later targeted checks may improve precision but are not required.

gaps.gap_description: 1640s/1649 first-use claims lack a visible primary quotation.
gaps.recommended_action: Label 1640s/1649 as dictionary claims only; do not use as confirmed text dates.

gaps.gap_description: Transport hub earliest evidence relies heavily on Ngram/OCR signals rather than high-confidence direct early attestations.
gaps.recommended_action: Use transport phrases as frequency/category evidence; avoid precise earliest-date claims unless doing a later targeted scrape.

gaps.gap_description: Hub-and-spoke has strong modern signal but noisy early Ngram years.
gaps.recommended_action: Anchor hub-and-spoke to modern system architecture rather than early first_nonzero_year.

gaps.gap_description: Digital/platform hub phrases have sparse or low-confidence snippet evidence.
gaps.recommended_action: Show digital/platform terms as later Ngram-supported extensions; avoid origin or dominance claims.

gaps.gap_description: Server hub and platform hub are sparse and should not carry major chart weight.
gaps.recommended_action: Exclude from primary compound comparison or place in a low-signal appendix group.

gaps.gap_description: Snippet sample count remains below the original broad target and uneven across categories.
gaps.recommended_action: Use snippets selectively for examples; do not make snippet-density charts.

gaps.gap_description: Nave relation is supported for context but not enough for a standalone origin argument.
gaps.recommended_action: Use nave as a small contextual note beside wheel terminology.
